title: Scientific Method
definition: The systematic process of observation, hypothesis formation, experimentation, and theory building that underlies all empirical science.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A step-by-step procedure scientists use to test their ideas and learn about the world around us.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The set of principles guiding how scientists ask questions, make predictions, design experiments, analyze data, and build theories based on evidence.
difficulty_levels.expert: A flexible yet rigorous process enabling scientific progress by ensuring empirical justification for claims, fostering the growth of knowledge, and maintaining its reliability through peer review and reproducibility.

extra_fields.insight: The power to challenge assumptions and drive advancement through evidence-based inquiry.
